 {"id":"bd-4l5","title":"bd prime: Detect ephemeral branches and adjust workflow output","description":"When 'bd prime' runs on a branch with no upstream (ephemeral branch), it should output a different SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL.

**Current output (wrong for ephemeral branches):**
```
[ ] 1. git status
[ ] 2. git add <files>
[ ] 3. bd sync
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."
[ ] 5. bd sync
[ ] 6. git push
```

**Needed output for ephemeral branches:**
```
[ ] 1. git status
[ ] 2. git add <files>
[ ] 3. bd sync --from-main  (pull updates from main)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."
[ ] 5. (no push - branch is ephemeral)
```

**Detection:** `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u}` returns error code 128 if no upstream.

Also update Sync & Collaboration section to mention `bd sync --from-main` for ephemeral branches.

**Use case:** Gastown polecats work on ephemeral local branches that are never pushed. Their code gets merged to main via local merge, and beads changes stay local (communicated via gm mail to Overseer).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-25T16:55:24.984104-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T17:12:46.604978-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:12:46.604978-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-4pv","title":"bd export only outputs 1 issue after auto-import corrupts database","description":"When auto-import runs and purges issues (due to git history backfill bug), subsequent 'bd export' only exports 1 issue even though the database should have many.

Reproduction:
1. Have issues.jsonl with 55 issues
2. Auto-import triggers and purges all issues via git history backfill
3. Run 'bd export' - only exports 1 issue (the last one created before corruption)

The database gets into an inconsistent state where most issues are purged but export doesn't realize this.

Workaround: Rebuild database from scratch with 'rm .beads/beads.db && bd init --prefix bd'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:40.828866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T22:57:28.37576-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:50:35.036227-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-4t7","title":"Auto-import runs during --no-auto-import operations via stats/ready commands","description":"Even when using --no-auto-import flag, certain commands like 'bd stats' and 'bd ready' still trigger auto-import internally, which can cause the git history backfill bug to corrupt data.

Example:
  bd stats --no-auto-import
  # Still prints 'Purged bd-xxx (recovered from git history...)'

The flag should completely disable auto-import for the command, but it appears some code paths still trigger it.

Workaround: Use --allow-stale instead, or --sandbox mode.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:59.305898-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T00:54:20.335013-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T00:54:12.561872-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-5bj","title":"Registry has cross-process race condition","description":"The global daemon registry (~/.beads/registry.json) can be corrupted when multiple daemons from different workspaces write simultaneously.

**Root cause:**
- Registry uses an in-process mutex but no file-level locking
- Register() and Unregister() release the mutex between read and write
- Multiple daemon processes can interleave their read-modify-write cycles

**Evidence:**
Found registry.json with double closing bracket: `]]` instead of `]`

**Fix options:**
1. Use file locking (flock/fcntl) around the entire read-modify-write cycle
2. Use atomic write pattern (write to temp file, rename)
3. Both (belt and suspenders)

**Files:**
- internal/daemon/registry.go:46-64 (readEntries)
- internal/daemon/registry.go:67-87 (writeEntries)
- internal/daemon/registry.go:90-108 (Register - the race window)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T13:55:50.426188-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T14:07:06.22622-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T14:07:06.22622-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-736d","title":"Refactor path canonicalization into helper function","description":"The path canonicalization logic (filepath.Abs + EvalSymlinks) is duplicated in 3 places:

- cmd/bd/main.go:446-451 (--no-db cleanup)

Refactoring suggestion:
Extract to a helper function like:

This would:
- Reduce code duplication
- Make the logic easier to maintain
- Ensure consistent behavior across all path handling

 {"id":"bd-c362","title":"Extract database search logic into helper function","description":"The logic for finding a database in a beads directory is duplicated:
- FindDatabasePath() BEADS_DIR section (beads.go:141-169)
- findDatabaseInTree() (beads.go:248-280)

Both implement the same search order:
1. Check config.json first (single source of truth)
2. Fall back to canonical beads.db
3. Search for *.db files, filtering backups and vc.db

Refactoring suggestion:
Extract to a helper function like:
  func findDatabaseInBeadsDir(beadsDir string) string

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for database search logic
- Easier to maintain and update search order
- Reduces code duplication

 {"id":"bd-c4rq","title":"Refactor: Move staleness check inside daemon branch","description":"## Problem

Currently ensureDatabaseFresh() is called before the daemon mode check, but it checks daemonClient != nil internally and returns early. This is redundant.

**Location:** All read commands (list.go:196, show.go:27, ready.go:102, status.go:80, etc.)

## Current Pattern

Call happens before daemon check, function checks daemonClient internally.

## Better Pattern

Move staleness check to direct mode branch only, after daemon check.

## Impact
Low - minor performance improvement (avoids one function call per command in daemon mode)

## Effort
Medium - requires refactoring 8 command files

## Priority
Low - can defer to future cleanup PR","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:17:45.119583-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-20T20:17:45.119583-05:00"}

1. Stop at git root (don't search above it)
2. Only use explicit BEADS_DB env var or local .beads
3. At minimum, don't search in home directory","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T22:10:41.992686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T22:10:41.992686-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-dmb","title":"Fresh clone: bd should suggest 'bd init' when no database exists","description":"On a fresh clone of a repo using beads, running `bd stats` or `bd list` gives a cryptic error:

```
Error: failed to open database: post-migration validation failed: migration invariants failed:
  - required_config_present: required config key missing: issue_prefix (database has 2 issues)
```

**Expected**: A helpful message like:
```
No database found. This appears to be a fresh clone.
Run 'bd init --prefix <prefix>' to hydrate from the committed JSONL file.
Found: .beads/beads.jsonl (38 issues)
```

**Why this matters**: The current UX is confusing for new contributors or fresh clones. The happy path should be obvious.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:04.947959-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:04.947959-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-e166","title":"Improve timestamp comparison readability in import","description":"The timestamp comparison logic uses double-negative which can be confusing:

Current code:
if !incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // skip update
}

More readable:
if incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // perform update
} else {
    // skip (local is newer)
}

This is a minor refactor for code clarity.

 {"id":"bd-ghb","title":"Add --yes flag to bd doctor --fix for non-interactive mode","description":"## Feature Request

Add a `--yes` or `-y` flag to `bd doctor --fix` that automatically confirms all prompts, enabling non-interactive usage in scripts and CI/CD pipelines.

## Current Behavior
`bd doctor --fix` prompts for confirmation before applying fixes, which blocks automated workflows.

## Desired Behavior
`bd doctor --fix --yes` should apply all fixes without prompting.

## Use Cases
- CI/CD pipelines that need to auto-fix issues
- Scripts that automate repository setup
- Pre-commit hooks that want to silently fix issues","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:22:45.486584-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:22:45.486584-08:00"}

-{"id":"bd-guc","title":"bd sync should not stage gitignored snapshot files","description":"## Problem

`gitCommitBeadsDir` in `cmd/bd/sync.go` runs `git add .beads/` which stages all files in the directory, including snapshot files that are listed in `.beads/.gitignore`.

If a snapshot file (e.g., `beads.left.meta.json`) was ever committed before being added to `.gitignore`, git continues to track it. This causes merge conflicts when multiple polecats run `bd sync` concurrently, since each one modifies and commits these temporary files.

## Root Cause

Line ~568 in sync.go:
```go
addCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "add", beadsDir)
```

This stages everything in `.beads/`, but `.gitignore` only prevents *untracked* files from being added - it doesn't affect already-tracked files.

## Suggested Fix

Option A: After `git add .beads/`, run `git reset` on snapshot files:
```go
exec.Command("git", "reset", "HEAD", ".beads/beads.*.jsonl", ".beads/*.meta.json")
```

Option B: Stage only specific files instead of the whole directory:
```go
exec.Command("git", "add", ".beads/issues.jsonl", ".beads/deletions.jsonl", ".beads/metadata.json")
```

Option C: Detect and untrack snapshot files if they're tracked:
```go
// Check if file is tracked: git ls-files --error-unmatch <file>
// If tracked, run: git rm --cached <file>
```

Option B is probably cleanest - explicitly add only the files that should be committed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:47:14.603799-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T22:34:23.336713-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:34:23.336713-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-guc","title":"bd sync should not stage gitignored snapshot files","description":"## Problem

`gitCommitBeadsDir` in `cmd/bd/sync.go` runs `git add .beads/` which stages all files in the directory, including snapshot files that are listed in `.beads/.gitignore`.

If a snapshot file (e.g., `beads.left.meta.json`) was ever committed before being added to `.gitignore`, git continues to track it. This causes merge conflicts when multiple polecats run `bd sync` concurrently, since each one modifies and commits these temporary files.

## Root Cause

Line ~568 in sync.go:
```go
addCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "add", beadsDir)
```

This stages everything in `.beads/`, but `.gitignore` only prevents *untracked* files from being added - it doesn't affect already-tracked files.

## Suggested Fix

Option A: After `git add .beads/`, run `git reset` on snapshot files:
```go
exec.Command("git", "reset", "HEAD", ".beads/beads.*.jsonl", ".beads/*.meta.json")
```

Option B: Stage only specific files instead of the whole directory:
```go
exec.Command("git", "add", ".beads/issues.jsonl", ".beads/deletions.jsonl", ".beads/metadata.json")
```

Option C: Detect and untrack snapshot files if they're tracked:
```go
// Check if file is tracked: git ls-files --error-unmatch <file>
// If tracked, run: git rm --cached <file>
```

Option B is probably cleanest - explicitly add only the files that should be committed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:47:14.603799-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T10:51:09.801928-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:34:23.336713-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-ho5","title":"Add 'town report' command for aggregated swarm status","description":"## Problem
Getting a full swarm status requires running 6+ commands:
- `town list <rig>` for each rig
- `town mail inbox` as Boss
- `bd list --status=open/in_progress` per rig

This is slow and error-prone for both humans and agents.

## Proposed Solution
Add `town report [RIG]` command that aggregates:
- All rigs with polecat states (running/stopped, awake/asleep)
- Boss inbox summary (unread count, recent senders)
- Aggregate issue counts per rig (open/in_progress/blocked)

Example output:
```
=== beads ===
Polecats: 5 (5 running, 0 stopped)
Issues: 20 open, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked

=== gastown ===
Polecats: 6 (4 running, 2 stopped)
Issues: 0 open, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked

=== Boss Mail ===
Unread: 10 | Total: 22
Recent: rictus (21:19), scrotus (21:14), immortanjoe (21:14)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `town report` shows all rigs
- [ ] `town report <rig>` shows single rig detail
- [ ] Output is concise and scannable
- [ ] Completes in <2 seconds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T22:55:36.8919-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T22:56:08.071838-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:56:08.071838-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-imj","title":"Deletion propagation via deletions manifest","description":"## Problem

When `bd cleanup -f` or `bd delete` removes issues in one clone, those deletions don't propagate to other clones. The import logic only creates/updates, never deletes. This causes "resurrection" where deleted issues reappear.

## Root Cause

Import sees DB issues not in JSONL and assumes they're "local unpushed work" rather than "intentionally deleted upstream."

## Solution: Deletions Manifest

Add `.beads/deletions.jsonl` - an append-only log of deleted issue IDs with metadata.

### Format
```jsonl
{"id":"bd-xxx","ts":"2025-11-25T10:00:00Z","by":"stevey"}
{"id":"bd-yyy","ts":"2025-11-25T10:05:00Z","by":"claude","reason":"duplicate of bd-zzz"}
```

### Fields
- `id`: Issue ID (required)
- `ts`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (required)
- `by`: Actor who deleted (required)
- `reason`: Optional context ("cleanup", "duplicate of X", etc.)

### Import Logic
```
For each DB issue not in JSONL:
  1. Check deletions manifest → if found, delete from DB
  2. Fallback: check git history → if found, delete + backfill manifest
  3. Neither → keep (local unpushed work)
```

### Conflict Resolution
Simultaneous deletions from multiple clones are handled naturally:
- Append-only design means both clones append their deletion records
- On merge, file contains duplicate entries (same ID, different timestamps)
- `LoadDeletions` deduplicates by ID (keeps any/first entry)
- Result: deletion propagates correctly, duplicates are harmless

### Pruning Policy
- Default retention: 7 days (configurable via `deletions.retention_days`)
- Auto-compact during `bd sync` is **opt-in** (disabled by default)
- Hard cap: `deletions.max_entries` (default 50000)
- Git fallback handles pruned entries (self-healing)

### Self-Healing
When git fallback catches a resurrection (pruned entry), it backfills the manifest. One-time git scan cost, then fast again.

### Size Estimates
- ~80 bytes/entry
- 7-day retention with 100 deletions/day = ~56KB
- Git compressed: ~10KB

## Benefits
- ✅ Deletions propagate across clones
- ✅ O(1) lookup (no git scan in normal case)
- ✅ Works in shallow clones
- ✅ Survives history rewrite
- ✅ Audit trail (who deleted what when)
- ✅ Self-healing via git fallback
- ✅ Bounded size via time-based pruning

## References
- Investigation session: 2025-11-25
- Related: bd-2q6d (stale database warnings)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:56:01.98027-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T16:36:27.965168-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T16:36:27.965168-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-qsm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:57:42.821911-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-x2i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:57:42.851712-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-44e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:57:42.88154-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-bhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:23.675787-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-bgs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:23.744648-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-f0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:23.80649-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-v29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:23.864569-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-mdw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:48.592492-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-imj","depends_on_id":"bd-03r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:54.295851-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
